title: tilia amurensis subsp. taquetii
intro: Tilia amurensis subsp. taquetii, also known as the Manchurian lime tree or Tilia taquetii, is a subspecies of Tilia amurensis, a deciduous tree native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The extracts from the tree have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits like soothing the respiratory system, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The herbal preparations of Tilia amurensis subsp. taquetii can be made into teas, infusions, and infusates, which are often used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and digestive issues.
